methods.why: Frozen baseline from Level 0/1; no new decoder work authorized.

methods.representation: Distributed sparse block codes (GSBC / block code factorizer)
why: Level 1F.3 cap saturation reflected our frozen cap=16 shootout policy, not the native envelope.
Upstream configs use uncapped or very high iteration budgets; native-envelope reproduction remains possible without modifying upstream.

methods.why: Previous audit already showed one flat codebook violates the current factor-specific domain contract.
Do not replicate during Level 3.0; reopen only if upstream code appears or a later isolated replication becomes necessary.

methods.minimal_boundary: Only the paper-defined encoder/decoder needed for U0/U1/U2 benchmark cells.
methods.paper_curve_test: Reproduce a paper-consistent exact/noisy recovery curve before any subject comparison.
methods.replacement_plan: Delete or replace the local reproduction if the authors release a suitable upstream implementation.

methods.why: Do not force into U1. Reopen only if U2 becomes a primary next-step target and no upstream implementation appears.
Use only as evaluator or tiny-domain reference, never as a practical latency claim.
Keep entirely separate from U0-U2 rankings.
